title: Show --external containers even without --all option
body:
We currently do not show --external containers when the user specifies
it, unless they also specify the --all flag. This has led to confusion.
I see no reason not to list them without the --all flag if the user
specifies the option.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12353

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

We've had this discussion before, and my position is still that this is even more confusing. We end up with a potentially large number of containers listed in `podman ps` that we cannot interact with in a meaningful way. `podman inspect`? No such container. `podman start`? No such container. Printing containers that we may know about, but are effectively unable to interact with outside of removal, seems just as bad.

Can we add a line at the end of the human-readable output - "There are X containers from Buildah also present on the system, use --external to show them" ? That should clue people in that there's something going on they need to worry about if the number starts climbing.

And we really need to do something about leaking build containers after a C-c on `podman build` - that's overwhelmingly the cause of this.
@mheon external containers are not displayed by default though. This PR only changes the requirement of --external to be used in conjunction with --all.

Ahhh. Clearly I need more coffee.

I'm starting to become attached to the idea of printing off the number of external containers if it's nonzero though. Seems sensible?
This PR LGTM to me regardless of that conversation

> I'm starting to become attached to the idea of printing off the number of external containers if it's nonzero though. Seems sensible?

I sympathize with the idea but we need to be careful to not break users who may want to parse the output.  Maybe print it unless --format is used?
Yeah, we shouldn't append to formatted output
